description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, two-story structure with a flat, weathered roof and a predominantly concrete exterior. The facade features large, grid-like orange metal-framed windows on the upper level, providing ample natural light and visibility. The lower level is more enclosed, with a combination of solid concrete walls and a few metal roll-up doors, likely for vehicle or livestock access. The exterior shows signs of aging and wear, with visible stains, discoloration, and some graffiti on the concrete surfaces. The building sits on a broad, flat concrete base, suggesting stability and durability. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function over form.
description: A weathered, two-story concrete cowshed with large orange-framed windows and a flat roof, showing signs of age and utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main facade with large orange-framed windows on the upper level and a wide, solid concrete base below.
description_view_1: Side view reveals a solid wall with minimal windows and two metal roll-up doors at the base, likely for access.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a mostly blank concrete wall with a single large window section on the left side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows a smaller window section on the upper level and a mostly solid, stained concrete wall below.
attribute_window_presence: large orange-framed grid windows on two sides, upper level
